user_message: so does that mean paid acquisition is viable if the average client is worth about 10000 in first-month gross profit and CAC is 1000?
assistant_message: Yes, this is viable enough to test paid acquisition, with guardrails. At $1,000 CAC and about $10,000 in first-month gross profit, the business is at CFA Level 3, meaning the first sale can fund acquisition very comfortably. I would treat that as permission to run a controlled paid acquisition test, not permission to scale spend blindly. The diagnostic check still points at gross margin as the next constraint because current margin is about 76.9%, a little below the 80% target. So my recommendation is: test paid acquisition with a capped budget and simple tracking by channel, while tightening delivery scope/pricing so the margin gets above 80% before aggressive scaling.
